brief_document.title: Dashboard hosting — serve cluster AU's static assets
brief_document.purpose: Host the monitoring dashboard. The dashboard UI is built by cluster AU; this brief serves its static asset bundle from the server and backs it with the same public API and WebSocket event feed every other client uses. There is no dashboard-specific API — the dashboard is just another first-class client.
brief_document.task: Serve cluster AU's built static asset bundle from a configured (or embedded) path via the axum HTTP transport, alongside the public gRPC/HTTP handlers (AW-009) and the WebSocket event feed (AW-010). Add no dashboard-specific API surface: the dashboard consumes exactly the public contract.

requirements.title: Serve the static asset bundle
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL serve cluster AU's built static dashboard asset bundle from a configured asset path (or an embedded bundle), mounted on the HTTP transport.
acceptance: dashboard/assets.rs serves the static bundle (index + assets) from the configured dashboard asset path (AW-006 config), or from an embedded bundle when configured to embed.
The asset path/embedding mode comes from config, with no hardcoded default path (CO11).
Requests for unknown asset routes fall through correctly (SPA fallback to index where appropriate) without shadowing the API routes.
A test asserts the bundle root is served and an API route is not shadowed by the asset handler.

requirements.title: No dashboard-specific API
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL back the dashboard solely with the same public gRPC/HTTP handlers (AW-009) and WebSocket event feed (AW-010) every other client uses; it SHALL NOT add a dashboard-specific API or privileged side channel.
acceptance: No API route, RPC, or WebSocket subscription exists that is reachable only by the dashboard; the dashboard uses the public contract (D10).
The dashboard's data and live feed come from the workflow-management handlers and the WebSocket stream, scoped by namespace via the same guard (CO9).
An inspection/review confirms no handler branches on a 'dashboard' caller to expose extra data.
A test asserts the dashboard's data routes are the same public routes a client SDK would call.

boundaries: SHALL NOT build the dashboard UI — that is cluster AU; this brief only serves its built assets.
SHALL NOT add a privileged dashboard API or bypass namespace scoping (D10, CO9).
SHALL NOT implement the event feed itself — it reuses AW-010.
verification: Inspection confirms no dashboard-only API surface exists.
No #[allow], #[expect], or #[ignore] directives in new code.

checklist.text: The server serves cluster AU's built static asset bundle from a configured (or embedded) path.
The dashboard is backed only by the same public gRPC/HTTP handlers and WebSocket feed every other client uses; there is no dashboard-specific API.

stories.text: As a dashboard, I want to be served by the server and backed by the same public API every client uses so that there is no privileged side channel to maintain.

constraints.text: No #[allow]/#[expect]/#[ignore] lint bypasses per CLAUDE.md; tests needing a running server gate at runtime (a *_TEST_URL env var with a logged skip), never #[ignore].
lib.rs/mod.rs are declarations and re-exports only.
aion-proto is a leaf crate: it depends on aion-core and external crates only — no aion, no aion-server, no store backend. Structural; must hold (per D2).
aion-server depends on aion, aion-proto, and a store backend among workspace crates; it contains no workflow execution logic — it delegates every durable decision to the Engine (per D5).
Wire data types (proto messages, streamed events, summaries) derive Serialize + Deserialize; config types that carry secrets are Deserialize-only (D4, project credential policy).
Library errors use thiserror (WireError in proto, ServerError in server); anyhow appears only in aion-server's top-level main. No unwrap/expect in library code; lock/stream poison handled explicitly.
No cross-namespace operation reaches the engine: namespace authorisation and scoping happen at the adapter boundary before any Engine call (per D8).
A slow or stalled WebSocket consumer is dropped with a typed lag error; per-connection buffers are bounded; the engine broadcast is never back-pressured by a subscriber (per D6).
No hardcoded operational defaults (ports, heartbeat window, buffer bound, timeouts): all from config; engine-owned defaults deferred to the engine (per CLAUDE.md 'no assumed defaults').
The wire Event/WorkflowFilter/WorkflowSummary/WorkflowStatus and ID types are aion-core's types, not re-declared wire clones (per D2).

resolved_context.intention: Build the network skin of Aion. The aion engine crate is transport-agnostic by design — no HTTP, gRPC, or WebSocket — so a CLI embeds it with nothing but a file-backed store. Teams running many services want a managed deployment: one binary clients and workers point at, that streams live workflow events to a dashboard, isolates tenants, and dispatches activity tasks to out-of-process workers in any language. That is aion-server, and the wire contract it speaks is aion-proto. aion-proto is a pure leaf-level contract — gRPC service definitions plus serde wire types — that the server, every client SDK (AL), and every worker SDK (AR) agree on, defined once so they never drift. aion-server is the deployable: it wraps a live Engine, translates wire requests into Engine calls, tails the engine's event broadcast to feed WebSocket subscribers, runs the server side of the remote-worker protocol, partitions everything by namespace, and serves the dashboard's API and static assets. It adds no execution logic — every durable decision still happens in aion.
